NASDAQ: MUZE — Separate trading commenced March 23, 2026

Partnering with the Next-Generation Tech Platform

Muzero Acquisition Corp is a $201 million Nasdaq-listed special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) established to identify, partner with and acquire technology-enabled businesses and accelerate their path to institutional-grade public markets.

Meet the Team SEC Filings ↗
$201.25M IPO Technology-Enabled Acquisitions Artificial Intelligence NASDAQ: MUZE Cayman Islands Exempted Co. IPO Closed Feb 2, 2026 CIK: 0002093484 $201.25M IPO Technology-Enabled Acquisitions Artificial Intelligence NASDAQ: MUZE Cayman Islands Exempted Co. IPO Closed Feb 2, 2026 CIK: 0002093484
About

Built to Acquire
the Future

Muzero Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ: MUZE) is a blank check company incorporated as a Cayman Islands exempted company and formed for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses.

Employing a sector-driven approach that leverages our technology and artificial intelligence experience, we intend to focus on businesses that are technology-enabled across any industry or geographical location.

We aim to partner with founders and management teams to build a public company positioned for long-term growth and institutional participation — targeting management teams, operating structures, and governance practices appropriate for the public markets.

Meet the Team Investor Resources
Investment Thesis

What We Look For

While we possess the flexibility to identify, merge with, and acquire target businesses across any geography and industry sector, we intend to employ a sector-driven approach that leverages our management team's domain expertise, operational experience, and proprietary networks.

We aim to partner with technology-enabled businesses, leveraging a partnership approach grounded in our team's multi-faceted background across private equity, venture capital, quantitative finance, and AI-driven technology operations.

We draw on decades of experience from leading global institutions including Warburg Pincus, Clearlake Capital Group, The Carlyle Group, Microsoft, Google, McKinsey & Company, JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Merrill Lynch and Morgan Stanley.

We look forward to working with compelling businesses within high-growth, technology-driven ecosystems.

IPO Details

Transaction
Overview

IPO closed February 2, 2026. Units listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under MUZEU. Separate trading of Class A ordinary shares (MUZE) and warrants (MUZEW) commenced March 23, 2026.

// Leadership

Our Team

Management
Von Lam
CEO · Director

Von Lam has over 20 years of experience across global technology venture capital, private equity, special situations credit and corporate development. He is the Founder and Managing Partner of Falcon Partners Limited (doing business as Increment Capital). He is also a Managing Member of Innomatrix LLC, a digital infrastructure real estate development company. Prior roles include Clearlake Capital Group, Warburg Pincus, UBS Asset Management, Acion Partners, Microsoft Corporate Development, and J.P. Morgan. He also served as an Independent Non-Executive Director of eCargo Holdings (ASX). A.B. Applied Mathematics & Economics, Harvard.

Yuming Zou
CFO

Yuming Zou has over 20 years of financial and executive leadership experience. He is currently Executive Director and Chief Financial Officer of Fangzhou Inc. (HKEX: 6086), where he oversees corporate finance, financial management, and investor relations. Prior to Fangzhou, he spent 15 years at J.P. Morgan Chase & Co., serving as a trader and Executive Director. He also served as an Independent Non-Executive Director of eCargo Holdings Limited, a company previously listed on the ASX. Mr. Zou is a Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) and holds an A.M in Statistics and an A.B. in Economics from Harvard.

Patrick Aber
COO · Director

Patrick Aber is the Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer of Tornado, an AI-powered financial research and analysis platform. He previously served as an Associate Portfolio Manager at The Carlyle Group following its acquisition of Vermillion Asset Management, and earlier held quantitative and investment roles at Tower Research Capital and Beecher Investors. He holds FINRA Series 4, 7, 24, and 63 licenses and an A.B. in Applied Mathematics and Economics from Harvard.

Steven Maksymyk
CSO

Steven Maksymyk is the Managing Partner of 229 Advisors, providing fractional executive and strategy consulting services. Previously, he held senior leadership roles at Sonder Holdings, Rocky Mountaineer, and InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG), where he led operations, partnerships, and global strategy initiatives. His earlier career includes investment banking and capital markets roles at Scotia Capital and Bank of Nova Scotia. Mr. Maksymyk is a Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) and holds a Bachelor of Commerce degree from the University of Manitoba.

Independent Directors
Sheldon Trainor-DeGirolamo
Chairman · Independent Director

Sheldon Trainor-DeGirolamo is the Founder and Managing Director of PacBridge Capital Partners and brings decades of experience in principal investment and investment banking. Before founding PacBridge in 2009, he spent two decades in high-level leadership roles, including with Credit Suisse Group AG Australia, Morgan Stanley Asia, as the Head of Investment Banking for Asia and as Vice Chairman of Merrill Lynch Asia. He has served on the boards of multiple public companies, including BitDeer Technologies Group, Foxconn Interconnect Technology, Macau Legend Development Company, and Athenex Inc. Mr. Trainor-DeGirolamo holds a Bachelor of Commerce degree from the University of British Columbia.

Hope Ni
Chair, Audit Committee · Independent Director

Hope Ni is an experienced investor and executive with over 15 years of public company board experience. She currently manages the Abax Family Office and serves as an independent director of several publicly listed companies, including Zhihu Inc., Acotec Scientific Holdings, Visen Pharmaceuticals, and ATA Creativity Global. Her prior experience includes investment banking at Merrill Lynch and legal practice at Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP. Ms. Ni holds a bachelor's degree from Cornell University and a Juris Doctor from the University of Pennsylvania Law School.

Derek Reisfield
Chair, Compensation Committee · Independent Director

Derek Reisfield is the Co-Founder and CEO of Edisource International, an AI content creation company, and a Senior Advisor to G2 Capital Advisors. He has held numerous executive roles, including CFO of Ondas Holdings and AllSci, and CEO of BBN Networks. His foundational experience includes co-founding MarketWatch, serving as President of CBS New Media, and leading media and telecommunications practices at McKinsey & Company and Mitchell Madison Group. He has sat on several public boards and is a director emeritus of the San Francisco Zoological Society. Mr. Reisfield holds a BA from Wesleyan University and an MA in Communications Management from the Annenberg School of Communications at USC.

Advisors
Gary Linscott
Advisor

Gary Linscott is a seasoned AI executive and researcher with deep experience in autonomy and large-scale ML platforms. As a published author in large-scale reinforcement learning with 68 patent applications, he is also known for Stockfish and LCZero, which have been the two world-leading chess engines for many years. He has held senior software engineering roles at DoorDash Labs, Zoox, Google (Cloud TPU and Bigtable), Goldman Sachs, and Microsoft. Mr. Linscott holds a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Queen's University.

Yiding (Frederick) He
Advisor

Principal at Evermo, the capital markets and advisory arm of Antalpha Platform Holding Co. (NASDAQ: ANTA), focusing on institutional financing and digital asset–related infrastructure. Former Director of Business Development at BITMAIN, managing strategic accounts across the US and Latin America. B.A. Public Relations, Boston University.

Press Releases

Latest News

March 20, 2026
Muzero Acquisition Corp Announces the Separate Trading of its Class A Ordinary Shares and Warrants, Commencing March 23, 2026

Holders of the units sold in the IPO may elect to separately trade the Company's Class A ordinary shares and warrants. Shares trade as MUZE and warrants as MUZEW on Nasdaq.
February 2, 2026
Muzero Acquisition Corp Announces Closing of $201,250,000 Initial Public Offering, Including Full Exercise of Underwriters' Over-Allotment Option

The Company closed its IPO of 20,125,000 units at $10.00 per unit, including full exercise of the 2,625,000 unit over-allotment, resulting in gross proceeds of $201,250,000.
January 29, 2026
Muzero Acquisition Corp Announces the Pricing of $175 Million Initial Public Offering

Muzero Acquisition Corp announced the pricing of its IPO of 17,500,000 units at $10.00 per unit. Units began trading on the Nasdaq Global Market as MUZEU on January 30, 2026.

10-K Mar 31, 2026 Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. Filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. View ↗

SCHEDULE 13D Feb 9, 2026 Schedule 13D beneficial ownership report filed with the SEC. View
8-K Feb 6, 2026 Closing of $201,250,000 IPO. 20,125,000 units at $10.00. Full exercise of 2,625,000 unit over-allotment. $201.25M placed into U.S. trust account (Continental Stock Transfer & Trust). View

424B4 Feb 2, 2026 Final prospectus (Rule 424B4) — Prospectus supplement for the IPO of 17,500,000 units at $10.00 per unit, with over-allotment option for 2,625,000 additional units. View
8-K Jan 29, 2026 Pricing of $175M IPO — 17,500,000 units at $10.00 per unit. Units begin trading on Nasdaq Global Market as MUZEU on January 30, 2026. View
EFFECT Jan 29, 2026 Notice of Effectiveness — Registration Statement declared effective by the SEC. View
